LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JORDAN CROWLEY'S MOTION FOR PARTIAL RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT INVISIBLE TECHNOLOGIES INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. DISCUSSIONS HELD. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JORDAN CROWLEY'S MOTION FOR PARTIAL RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. CROWLEY'S INDIVIDUAL AND REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS ARE STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. DEPUTY: NOT PRESENT. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Continued To Jan-15-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Partial Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendant Invisible Technologies Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-13-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Jeffrey Jackson, Esq., Tel. 213.537.9225, appearing for plaintiff Jordan Crowley;
Carmen Miranda, Esq., Tel. 310.434.5492, appearing for defendant Invisible Technologies, Inc.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF JORDAN CROWLEY's Motion For Partial Reconsideration Of Order Granting Defendant Invisible Technologies Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

The matter is called and passed.

At the second call of the matter, discussions only are held. At the conclusion of discussions, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Jordan Crowley's motion for partial reconsideration of the order granting the motion to compel arbitration is granted. Crowley's individual and representative PAGA claims are stayed pending the completion of the arbitration.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Not present. Matter not reported.
(302/RBU)
